content.full_text:
Diamond Bar Gadfly Association
23958 Golden Springs Dr., Diamond Bar, CA 91765 * (714) 861-8898, FAX: 861-8881

December 10, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
Attn: Ms. Alice Hughs, Councilor
428 J Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807


Dear Ms. Hughs:

We have been advised to contact your agency in order to determine whether our informal Gadfly Association of friends and neighbors is required to register with the Fair Political Practices Commission.

Until recently we have served as an informal association of Diamond Bar residents with no membership roster. We attend council meetings and monitor city activities. We neither maintain bank accounts nor collect dues or any other contributions, or conduct fund raisers of any kind. (Newspaper clipping enclosed.)

The point at issue is whether or not a video, produced by Diamond Bar residents and given to our group for free community distribution, with credits to the Gadfly Association constitutes an in-kind contribution. It is our perception that while the tape is very professional, both in production quality and scripting, it is still a product produced by fellow citizens on their spare time and with their own equipment, and the production costs thereof would be negligible.

The video neither supports a candidate or any election issue, though it is defiantly critical of City Government (no individuals are mentioned). It features a General Plan, and identifies several issues we feel are of vital concern to the community.

Your immediate attention to this matter is greatly appreciated, and of course we will comply with what ever advice you offer, however we sincerely believe we are not too far removed from those casual video photographers who happen to witness a news event and take "John Q Citizen" type pictures. Please respond as soon as possible.

Yours,

Al Rumpilla

Snapshot 12-10-91 TRIBUNE

His role is champion
for the common citizen

Since Diamond Bar incorporated in April 1988, Al Rumpilla hasn't missed a City Council meeting.

Perfect attendance at council meetings is a claim most council members can't even make, but Rumpilla is a dedicated — arguably the most dedicated — observer of Diamond Bar's local government.

"I'm on a fixed income and I don't want to be forced out of Diamond Bar, for financial reasons or any other reasons," Rumpilla said.

Despite the city's reputation for being wealthy, Rumpilla maintains that most Diamond Bar residents are in the middle-income range. He sees himself as their defender against special interests.

Rumpilla, who is disabled, said he will keep attending council meetings as long as his health allows him to do so.

"If someone like me doesn't look after the city, then someone else could be run out of town," he said.

Rumpilla, who headed the organization which opposed incorporation, now heads a different group: the Diamond Bar

Gadfly Association, a watchdog operation that looks over the city.

"I know people think that a lot of what I say and do is a slap at the city, but that's not true. Now that we're a city, I want us to be the best city we can be."

— JOSEPH ASCENZI

sections.question: The point at issue is whether or not a video, produced by Diamond Bar residents and given to our group for free community distribution, with credits to the Gadfly Association constitutes a "in-kind contribution". It is our perception that while the tape is very professional, both in production quality and scripting, it is still a product produced by fellow citizens on their spare time and with their own equipment, and the production costs therefore would be negligible.
sections.question_synthetic: Must the Gadfly Association register with the FPPC, and does a video produced by residents and distributed by the group with the group's credit constitute an in-kind contribution requiring reporting?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; question and summary extracted from body text. Document contains newspaper clipping attachment. Requesting guidance on registration requirements and in-kind contribution reporting.

embedding.summary: An incoming request from the Gadfly Association of Diamond Bar asking whether they must register with the FPPC and whether a video produced by residents and distributed with the group's credit constitutes an in-kind contribution.
